attrs.alt: Terms of Use Conditional Access gate: sign-in triggers CA policy, checks acceptance, presents PDF if not accepted, records acceptance with timestamp

content.text: What Terms of Use Provides

Terms of Use (ToU) in Entra ID Governance is a Conditional Access grant control that forces users to read and accept a policy document — displayed as an inline PDF — before they can access a specified application or resource. Acceptance is recorded in the Entra audit log with a timestamp, user identity, and the version of the document they accepted. This creates a legally defensible acceptance record for compliance with GDPR data processing consent, employee acceptable-use policies, confidentiality agreements for external partners, and regulatory access requirements.
Inline PDF: the document is displayed in the browser and the user must scroll through it before the Accept button becomes clickable (if requireReadConsent is enabled).

Version tracking: when you publish a new version of a ToU document, users who previously accepted the old version are presented with the new version on their next sign-in. Acceptance history records which version each user accepted.
Per-device re-acceptance: optionally require users to accept the ToU on each new device or browser, preventing shared-device bypass.
Expiry: acceptance can expire after a configured number of days, requiring periodic re-acceptance to confirm ongoing consent.
Creating a Terms of Use Agreement

A ToU agreement is an Entra object holding the PDF document and acceptance settings. You attach it to one or more Conditional Access policies as a grant control. The agreement itself does not enforce access — the CA policy does. The ToU agreement is just the content and configuration.

# Upload a Terms of Use agreement via Graph API
# Requires Agreement.ReadWrite.All or Policy.ReadWrite.ConditionalAccess

# Read the PDF and base64-encode it
$pdfPath  = './acceptable-use-policy-v2.pdf'
$pdfBytes = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes($pdfPath)
$pdfB64   = [System.Convert]::ToBase64String($pdfBytes)

$body = @{
    displayName               = 'Acceptable Use Policy v2'
    isViewingBeforeAcceptanceRequired = $true
    isPerDeviceAcceptanceRequired     = $false
    userReacceptRequiredFrequency     = 'P365D'
    files = @(
        @{
            fileName  = 'acceptable-use-policy-v2.pdf'
            language  = 'en'
            isDefault = $true
            fileData  = @{
                data = $pdfB64
            }
        }
    )
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 6

$agreement = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/agreements' \
  -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json'

Write-Host "Agreement ID: $($agreement.id)"
ToU Agreement Settings
Setting
Effect
isViewingBeforeAcceptanceRequired
When true, the user must scroll to the bottom of the PDF before the Accept button is enabled. Prevents checkbox-clicking without reading.
isPerDeviceAcceptanceRequired
When true, acceptance is tracked per device. A user signing in from a new device must re-accept. Useful for shared workstation environments.
userReacceptRequiredFrequency
ISO 8601 duration (P365D = annually). After this period, the acceptance expires and the user is prompted again. null means acceptance never expires.
files[].language
IETF language tag (en, fr, de). Multiple language files can be uploaded; Entra presents the file matching the user's browser language, falling back to the default.
Enforcing ToU with a Conditional Access Policy

A ToU agreement has no enforcement on its own. You must create or update a Conditional Access policy that uses the agreement as a grant control. The scope of the CA policy determines who is shown the ToU and for which applications.

Test the CA policy with Report-Only mode before enabling it. Use the What-If tool in the Azure portal (Conditional Access > What If) to simulate the sign-in of a specific guest user and confirm the ToU grant appears in the evaluated policies.
Multi-Language ToU Documents

A single agreement can contain multiple PDF files, one per supported language. When a user sign-in lands on the ToU presentation page, Entra selects the PDF whose language matches the user's browser Accept-Language header. If no match is found, the file marked isDefault=true is shown.

# Add a French-language version to an existing agreement
$agreementId = '<agreement-id>'
$frPdfBytes  = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('./acceptable-use-policy-v2-fr.pdf')
$frPdfB64    = [System.Convert]::ToBase64String($frPdfBytes)

$frFile = @{
    fileName  = 'acceptable-use-policy-v2-fr.pdf'
    language  = 'fr'
    isDefault = $false
    fileData  = @{ data = $frPdfB64 }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 4

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/agreements/$agreementId/files" \
  -Body $frFile -ContentType 'application/json'
Querying Acceptance Status
The acceptances endpoint on each agreement lists every user who has accepted, with the timestamp, device state, and expiry. This is the audit evidence used for compliance reporting.

# List all acceptances for a ToU agreement
$agreementId = '<agreement-id>'
$acceptances = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/agreements/$agreementId/acceptances"

$acceptances.value | Select-Object `
    @{N='User';     E={$_.userPrincipalName}},
    @{N='Accepted'; E={$_.recordedDateTime}},
    @{N='Expires';  E={$_.expirationDateTime}},
    @{N='State';    E={$_.state}},
    @{N='DeviceId'; E={$_.deviceId}}

# Export for compliance audit
$acceptances.value | Export-Csv -Path "~/tou-acceptances-$(Get-Date -Format yyyyMMdd).csv" -NoTypeInformation

# Check if a specific user has a current (non-expired) acceptance
$userUpn = 'alice@contoso.com'
$current = $acceptances.value | Where-Object {
    $_.userPrincipalName -eq $userUpn -and
    $_.state -eq 'accepted' -and
    ($_.expirationDateTime -eq $null -or [datetime]$_.expirationDateTime -gt (Get-Date))
}
Write-Host "Current acceptance: $($current -ne $null)"
Revoking and Re-presenting ToU

You can revoke a user's acceptance, which forces them to re-accept the ToU on their next sign-in even if it has not expired. This is used when a user's role changes and they need to acknowledge a different version, or after an investigation requires confirmation of current consent.

# Revoke a specific user's acceptance
$agreementId  = '<agreement-id>'
$acceptanceId = '<acceptance-id>'

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method DELETE \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/agreements/$agreementId/acceptances/$acceptanceId"

# The user will be shown the ToU again on their next sign-in

# Revoke ALL acceptances for an agreement (re-present to everyone on next sign-in)
# Useful when publishing a major revision that everyone must re-read
$all = (Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/agreements/$agreementId/acceptances").value

foreach ($a in $all) {
    Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method DELETE \
      -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/agreements/$agreementId/acceptances/$($a.id)"
}
Write-Host "Revoked $($all.Count) acceptances"
Common Patterns
Scenario
Configuration
All employees must accept annual AUP
ToU on CA policy targeting all member users and all cloud apps. userReacceptRequiredFrequency=P365D.
B2B guests accept data processing notice
Separate ToU agreement with GDPR language. CA policy scoped to guest userType only.
Contractors accept confidentiality agreement on each device
isPerDeviceAcceptanceRequired=true. CA policy scoped to contractor security group.
New ToU version — everyone must re-read
Upload new PDF as a new file version. Revoke all existing acceptances via Graph API.
Emergency responders bypass ToU during incident
Add emergency access (break-glass) accounts to a named location or exclusion group in the CA policy.
Audit Log Queries

// All ToU acceptances in the last 30 days
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName == 'Agree to terms of use'
| extend User      = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend Agreement = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend Device    = tostring(AdditionalDetails[?(@.key=='deviceId')].value)
| project TimeGenerated, User, Agreement, Device
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// Users who declined in the last 7 days
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OperationName == 'Decline terms of use'
| extend User = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| project TimeGenerated, User, ResultDescription
Version Management

Each time you upload a new PDF file to an agreement, a new file version is created. Entra tracks version history and which version each user accepted. Publishing a new version does not automatically revoke existing acceptances — you must decide whether to revoke them or let them expire naturally based on the significance of the change.

# List existing file versions for an agreement
$agreementId = '<agreement-id>'
$files = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/agreements/$agreementId/files"

$files.value | Select-Object id, fileName, language, isDefault, createdDateTime

# Add a new version of the English PDF (replaces the default)
$newPdfBytes = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('./aup-v3.pdf')
$newPdfB64   = [System.Convert]::ToBase64String($newPdfBytes)

# First: get the existing English file ID
$enFile = $files.value | Where-Object { $_.language -eq 'en' }

# Update the file with new content by replacing the fileData
$patch = @{
    fileData = @{ data = $newPdfB64 }
    fileName = 'aup-v3.pdf'
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 4

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PATCH \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/agreements/$agreementId/files/$($enFile.id)" \
  -Body $patch -ContentType 'application/json'

# After uploading, decide whether to revoke all existing acceptances
# For minor changes (typos): leave acceptances intact
# For major changes (new obligations): revoke all so everyone re-reads
Monitoring with Microsoft Sentinel

ToU acceptance events flow into Log Analytics via the Entra diagnostic settings. In Microsoft Sentinel, these events can trigger analytics rules — for example, alerting when a high-privilege user declines the ToU, which may indicate they are attempting to avoid audit recording, or when acceptance patterns change suddenly.

// Sentinel analytics rule: alert when a Global Admin declines ToU
let globalAdmins = (AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where OperationName == 'Add member to role'
    | where TargetResources[0].displayName == 'Global Administrator'
    | extend AdminUpn = tostring(TargetResources[1].userPrincipalName)
    | summarize by AdminUpn);
AuditLogs
| where OperationName == 'Decline terms of use'
| extend User = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| where User in (globalAdmins)
| project TimeGenerated, User, ResultDescription

// Track ToU acceptance completion rate per agreement
// (useful for compliance dashboard: % of users who have accepted)
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where OperationName in ('Agree to terms of use', 'Decline terms of use')
| extend User      = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend Agreement = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend Decision  = iff(OperationName == 'Agree to terms of use', 'Accepted', 'Declined')
| summarize Count=count() by Agreement, Decision
ToU in Conditional Access Report-Only Mode

Before enforcing a new ToU policy, use CA Report-Only mode to measure its impact without blocking access. In Report-Only mode, sign-ins are evaluated against the policy but the grant control is not enforced. Sign-in logs record whether the policy would have required ToU acceptance, letting you identify users who would be prompted.
License Requirements
Feature
Required License
Create and manage ToU agreements
Entra ID P1 or P2 (or Microsoft 365 E3/E5)
Enforce ToU via Conditional Access
Entra ID P1 or P2
Per-device acceptance tracking
Entra ID P1 or P2
Multi-language ToU files
Entra ID P1 or P2
Acceptance reporting via Graph API
No additional license — included with ToU feature
Maximum agreements per tenant
Unlimited
Maximum file size per PDF
2 MB

ToU requires Entra ID P1 as a minimum. If you need P1 only for ToU and not for other P1 features, consider whether the compliance value justifies the per-user license cost versus alternative contract management solutions.
Summary
Terms of Use presents a PDF document as a Conditional Access grant control, recording user acceptance with timestamp and version in the Entra audit log for compliance evidence.
ToU agreements are enforced by CA policies — the agreement itself defines content and settings, while the CA policy defines who sees it, for which apps, and under what conditions.
isViewingBeforeAcceptanceRequired forces users to scroll through the full document before accepting; userReacceptRequiredFrequency sets how long an acceptance remains valid before re-presentation.
Multi-language support: upload one PDF per locale; Entra selects the file matching the user's browser language with a fallback to the default file.
Revoke acceptances programmatically via the Graph agreements/acceptances API — used when publishing a new version that requires all users to re-read and re-accept.
Audit evidence: query the Entra audit log for 'Agree to terms of use' and 'Decline terms of use' events, or export the acceptances endpoint as a CSV for compliance submissions.
